I've got thin long hair that I wear pulled back. I don't like of using heavy hair gels, and this littlllextra neatfix hair finishing stick makes for a great alternative. It holds all of the stray hairs in place without feeling heaviness. Plus, it’s really easy to use thanks to the wand/brush. Additionally, it held my hair in place for the majority of the day!

As far as your hair goes based on this video I'd say creams should be your go to. The loose "flowy" styles work well with you. 
As for me, I'm a paste and sometimes clay kind of guy for casual days. Lately though I've been loving Crystal Lake from Shear Revival.

Great video, there are so many hair products for sale, these 'back to basics' like videos really help to show what the differences are. 🍚🍙💷

I use water-based ; pomades, clays, creams, then I use either my fine or wide tooth comb to do a side part, then I use hairspray, because I'm someone who doesn't like to keep touching or looking at how my hair is throughout the day, so I use it to make sure it is in the same position as how I left it in. 🚿

Great video Robin. Pomades are my go to, but nowadays there's so much versatility within the pomade world that the lines have been blurred. We have traditional oil based (Murrays, Lockharts), traditional water based (Suavecito), unorthodox water based (flagship, grim grease, nostalgic grooming s/wb), but it's a huge reason why I love it. I got my hair cut recently and I'm rocking a shorter more textured look and have been using waxes and clays or a cream pomade like Gray Ghost by Shear Revival. Excellent stuff.
Like that you have a full head of hair now(sides grown out). Looks better on you Robin. Great video.👍

I think gel is something all guys have no idea how to use properly... If you have gel on ur hair and it is hard and rigid, you are using it wrongly. There is an extra step u need to do, that is to ruff up ur hair after it has dried and set. You will realize ur hair becomes soft and malleable again but the shape is still there, it should feel the same as if u applied soft clay or wax.

mixed_signals.text:
Last on my list of things to try is cream. I'm hoping this is the one. I'm super dumb with hair, it's really not my forte. I have similar hair to Robin. Gel was a no-go as it left my hair solid and too "wet" looking. Clay seemed to be too dry on my hair, and it is a bit hard to get out(at least the one I tried). Pomade was very much like gel, and like Robin said, it isn't good for every day. It seemed to make my hair look oily but dry when washed out. Praying for cream to be the miracle I need! 🙏 

Really appreciate your videos, they are huge for clueless folks like me!

Last on my list of things to try is cream. I'm hoping this is the one. I'm super dumb with hair, it's really not my forte. I have similar hair to Robin. Gel was a no-go as it left my hair solid and too "wet" looking. Clay seemed to be too dry on my hair, and it is a bit hard to get out(at least the one I tried). Pomade was very much like gel, and like Robin said, it isn't good for every day. It seemed to make my hair look oily but dry when washed out. Praying for cream to be the miracle I need!  

Really appreciate your videos, they are huge for clueless folks like me!

Personally, for thick hair, I would use an oil-based pomade. If your hair is super thick, gel would work great too. Gel doesn't work for a lot of people becuase it crunches your hair down, but if you have thick hair it should work. I'm a barber in the military and both pomade and gel work a miracle, but again, my hair is very thick and full. If you're looking for a poofy hairstyle I wouldn't recommend pomade or gel, though. I would recommend clay. But to answer your question, yes, pomade works wonderfully on thick hair, but keep in mind, it can't be sitting up super high, whereas with clay, it can. For a casual look with pomade I would go for a lighter hold, but for a more sleek, crisp look I would go with a medium to even a strong hold. But that's all up to you. Hope this helped. Cheers.
